---
title: "Gonorrhea Surveillance and Trend Analysis, United States 2000–2022"
subtitle: "01 — Data Exploration"
author: "[Your Name]"
date: "2026-03-03"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    theme: flatly
    highlight: tango
    df_print: kable
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo    = TRUE,
  message = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  fig.width  = 8,
  fig.height = 5,
  dpi     = 300
)
```

## 1. Load Libraries

```{r libraries}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(tidyr)
library(scales)
```

## 2. Load Raw Data

```{r load-data}
raw_data <- read_csv("gonorrhea_us_2000_2022.csv",
                     col_types = cols(
                       year       = col_integer(),
                       sex        = col_character(),
                       age_group  = col_character(),
                       cases      = col_integer(),
                       rate       = col_double(),
                       population = col_integer()
                     ))

cat("Dimensions:", dim(raw_data), "\n")
cat("Years covered:", paste(sort(unique(raw_data$year)), collapse = ", "), "\n")
cat("Sex categories:", paste(unique(raw_data$sex), collapse = ", "), "\n")
cat("Age groups:", paste(unique(raw_data$age_group), collapse = ", "), "\n")
cat("\nMissing values per column:\n")
print(colSums(is.na(raw_data)))
```

## 3. Clean and Process Data

```{r clean-data}
gonorrhea_clean <- raw_data %>%
  mutate(
    sex = factor(sex, levels = c("Male", "Female")),
    age_group = factor(age_group,
                       levels = c("15-19", "20-24", "25-29", "30-34",
                                  "35-44", "45-54", "55-64", "65+")),
    rate_log = log10(rate + 0.001)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(rate), rate >= 0)
```

### Summary Statistics: Case Rate per 100,000

```{r summary-by-sex}
gonorrhea_clean %>%
  group_by(sex) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_rate   = mean(rate, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_rate = median(rate, na.rm = TRUE),
    min_rate    = min(rate, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_rate    = max(rate, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups     = "drop"
  )
```

### Rates by Year (Both Sexes Combined)

```{r summary-by-year}
gonorrhea_clean %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  summarise(
    total_cases   = sum(cases, na.rm = TRUE),
    weighted_rate = sum(cases) / sum(population) * 1e5,
    .groups = "drop"
  )
```

```{r save-data, include=FALSE}
write_csv(gonorrhea_clean, "dgonorrhea_clean.csv")
```

## 4. Visualization

```{r color-palette, include=FALSE}
dir.create("output/figures", recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create("output/reports",  recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

sex_colors <- c("Male" = "#2166ac", "Female" = "#d6604d")
```

### Figure 1: Overall Trend by Sex

```{r fig1-trend-by-sex}
annual_by_sex <- gonorrhea_clean %>%
  group_by(year, sex) %>%
  summarise(
    total_cases = sum(cases),
    total_pop   = sum(population),
    agg_rate    = total_cases / total_pop * 1e5,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

p1 <- ggplot(annual_by_sex, aes(x = year, y = agg_rate, color = sex, group = sex)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5) +
  scale_color_manual(values = sex_colors, name = "Sex") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(2000, 2022, by = 3)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  labs(
    title    = "Gonorrhea Case Rates by Sex, United States, 2000–2022",
    subtitle = "Cases per 100,000 population",
    x        = "Year",
    y        = "Rate per 100,000",
    caption  = "Source: CDC STI Surveillance Data"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position  = "bottom",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

p1
ggsave("output/figures/trend_by_sex.png", plot = p1, width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 300)
```

### Figure 2: Heatmap by Age Group and Year

```{r fig2-heatmap-age-year, fig.width=9, fig.height=5.5}
age_year_rate <- gonorrhea_clean %>%
  group_by(year, age_group) %>%
  summarise(
    agg_rate = sum(cases) / sum(population) * 1e5,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

p2 <- ggplot(age_year_rate, aes(x = year, y = age_group, fill = agg_rate)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.3) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low      = "#f7fbff",
    mid      = "#6baed6",
    high     = "#08306b",
    midpoint = 200,
    name     = "Rate\nper 100k",
    labels   = comma
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(2000, 2022, by = 3)) +
  labs(
    title    = "Gonorrhea Rates by Age Group, United States, 2000–2022",
    subtitle = "Cases per 100,000 population (both sexes combined)",
    x        = "Year",
    y        = "Age Group",
    caption  = "Source: CDC STI Surveillance Data"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title  = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    panel.grid  = element_blank()
  )

p2
ggsave("output/figures/trend_by_age.png", plot = p2, width = 9, height = 5.5, dpi = 300)
```

### Figure 3: Most Recent Year by Age Group and Sex

```{r fig3-bar-latest-year, fig.width=9}
latest_year <- max(gonorrhea_clean$year)

p3 <- gonorrhea_clean %>%
  filter(year == latest_year) %>%
  group_by(age_group, sex) %>%
  summarise(rate = mean(rate), .groups = "drop") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = age_group, y = rate, fill = sex)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge", width = 0.7) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = sex_colors, name = "Sex") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  labs(
    title    = paste0("Gonorrhea Case Rates by Age Group and Sex, ", latest_year),
    subtitle = "Cases per 100,000 population",
    x        = "Age Group",
    y        = "Rate per 100,000",
    caption  = "Source: CDC STI Surveillance Data"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position  = "bottom",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

p3
ggsave("output/figures/rates_by_age_sex_latest.png", plot = p3, width = 9, height = 5, dpi = 300)
```

---

*Data Exploration Complete*
